div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: No

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Yes

display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Do you have a complex sample survey design object created with either svydesign() or svrepdesign() ?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Rather than convey, consider the laeken or vardpoor R libraries.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Do you want to analyze poverty, inequality, or richness/affluence?

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Poverty

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: I would like to estimate some summary indicators.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: I would like to estimate some useful graphs.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: survey::svycdf can be a useful tool to analyze 1st order poverty dominance.

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Main poverty measures

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyfgt( ... , g = 0) computes the headcount poverty rate,
a score unaffected by being
$1 or $100 below the poverty line

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyfgt( ... , g = 1) computes the
average normalized poverty gap,
which takes into account the distance to the poverty line:
people $100 below the poverty line have a
higher poverty score than those
$10 below the poverty line.
However, there is still an ethical problem:
distance to the poverty line is a linear function.
This means that if you take money
from the poorest and give it to
the less poor (without crossing
the poverty line),
the poverty measure is still the same

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyfgt( ... , g = 2) computes the
average squared normalized poverty gap,
where the above regressive transfer
will result in an increase of the
poverty measure. In other words,
gamma=2 takes 'inequality in poverty' into account

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svywatts() computes a measure comparable
to the average squared normalized poverty gap
with a time-to-exit-poverty interpretation:
under some (unrealistic, but useful) assumptions,
dividing the Watts measure by an income annual growth rate R
yields the average number of years
for leaving poverty status.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: svyfgtdec( ... , g = 2) decomposes the measure into incidence, intensity and inequality in poverty. Useful to analyze the differences across domains.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: svywattsdec() decomposes the measure into incidence, intensity and inequality in poverty. Useful to analyze the differences across domains.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Median-based poverty threshold

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyarpt() computes the threshold of a low standard
of living in comparison to the general living standards
of those in your survey dataset
(called the at-risk-of-poverty threshold)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Laeken poverty indicators

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
'How many are there overall?'
svyarpr() computes the share of the population
living below the at-risk-of-poverty threshold

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
'How do the elderly compare to everyone else?'
svyrmir() computes the
ratio of median income of
people above vs below a certain age

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
'How big is the gap, in terms of dollars (or other currency)?'
svyrmpg() computes the relative difference
between the median income of people below
the at-risk-of-poverty threshold
and the threshold itself

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
'How much income does the median number of this at-risk-of-poverty group have?'
svypoormed() computes the median income among individuals
living below the at-risk-of-poverty threshold

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Inequality

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: I would like to estimate some useful graphs.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svylorenz() provides a straightforward graphic of how
your population of interest differs from perfect equality

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: I would like to estimate some summary indicators.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Laeken inequality indicators

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygpg() computes the relative difference
of income between men and women

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyqsr() computes the ratio of income earned
by the top 20% against the income of the bottom 20%

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Main inequality measures

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Based on the Lorenz curve

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygini() computes the Gini coefficient,
a commonly-used value to describe
economic inequality that weights outliers equally.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyzenga() computes the Zenga measure,
a measure based on a transformation of the Lorenz curve.

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Based on Entropy

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygei( ... , epsilon = 0 ) computes the
Theil-L index, a measure sensitive to
differences among those with lower incomes

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygei( ... , epsilon = 1 ) computes the
Theil-T index, a measure sensitive to
differences among those with higher incomes

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyjdiv() computes the J-divergence measure
of inequality, a measure not
overly sensitive to either tail

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygeidec( ... , epsilon = 0 ) decomposes the
Theil-L index into inequality within and between groups.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svygeidec( ... , epsilon = 1 ) decomposes the
Theil-T index into inequality within and between groups.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyjdivdec() decomposes the
J-Divergence measure into inequality within and between groups.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Based on Social Welfare Functions

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyatk() computes the Atkinson index, an inequality measure directly related to the marginal utility of money.
This calculation expresses the idea that more money provides more welfare, but at a diminishing rate.
So $100 makes less difference in someone's welfare as they move up the income distribution.

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Richness/Affluence

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Summary measures

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Related to Polarization.
The concave, polarization-sensitive richness measures
increase with a progressive transfer among the rich

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyrich( ... , type_measure = 'Cha' ) is the Chakravarty richness measure.
As the g parameter goes to infinity, this measure tends towards the richness headcount ratio.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
svyrich( ... , type_measure = 'FGTT1' ) is the concave (for g < 1) FGT-like richness measure.
As the g parameter goes to 0, this measures tends towards the richness headcount ratio.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Related to Inequality.
The convex, inequality-sensitive richness measures
decline after a
progressive transfer among the rich.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: svyrich( ... , type_measure = 'FGTT2' ) is the convex (for g > 1) FGT-like richness measure.

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: Graphical Tools

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
survey::svycdf() can be used to visualize the fraction
of the population above (and below) a range of richness thresholds.
